zh.lifeSummary: 當Guy II de Saint Pol於1196年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯沙吉波爾-素-特爾諾時，他的父親Gaucher III de CHATILLON30歲，母親Elisabeth de SAINT-POL16歲。他和Agnès de Donzy II至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1226年8月13日在法國普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾瓦歐可盧斯阿維尼享年30歲，埋葬在法國瑟-吉馬內庫易利-朋吉奧-達斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guy II de Saint Pol于1196年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯沙吉波爾-素-特爾諾时，他的父亲Gaucher III de CHATILLON30岁，母亲Elisabeth de SAINT-POL16岁。他和Agnès de Donzy II至少有1个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1226年8月13日在法国普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾瓦歐可盧斯阿維尼享年30岁，埋葬在法国瑟-吉馬內庫易利-朋吉奧-達斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Pol
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Guillermo, Manuel, Jose, Juan, Rafael, Abelardo, Adolfo, Ana, Angel, Beatriz, Fernando, Gilberto.
Catalan and French (mainly northern, also Occitan): from the personal name Pol, equivalent to Paul .
Dutch and North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a grassy mound, from Middle Dutch pol ‘tussock’.

variants.name: Polus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois.
Polish (Poluś): from a pet form of the personal name Pol .
French: from a Latinized form of   Paul  .

variants.name: Polek
meanings: Polish: from a pet form of the personal name Pol (see Pol 5).

variants.name: Poll
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Heinz, Gerda, Gerhard, Kurt, Otto.
Dutch: variant of   Pol   2.
English (Norfolk): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Guy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of a compound name starting with witu ‘wood’ or wīt ‘wide’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England. In Old French initial w- regularly became gu-. The usual Norman forms of the name were Gy or Guido. In medieval Latin the same name is found as Wido. It was a popular name among the Normans, enhanced no doubt by the romance of Guy of Warwick, recounting the exploits of a folk hero of the Crusades.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

en.lifeSummary: When Guy II de Saint Pol was born in 1196, in Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, his father, Gaucher III de CHATILLON, was 30 and his mother, Elisabeth de SAINT-POL, was 16. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Agnès de Donzy II. He died on 13 August 1226, in Avignon, Vaucluse, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, at the age of 30, and was buried in Couilly-Pont-aux-Dames, Seine-et-Marne, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guy II de Saint Pol est né en 1196, à Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Gaucher III de CHATILLON, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth de SAINT-POL, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Agnès de Donzy II. Il est décédé le 13 août 1226, à Avignon, Vaucluse, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterré à Couilly-Pont-aux-Dames, Seine-et-Marne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guy II de Saint Pol 1196 in Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gaucher III de CHATILLON 30 und seine Mutter Elisabeth de SAINT-POL 16. Er hatte mit Agnès de Donzy II mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 13. August 1226 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Avignon, Vaucluse, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Frankreich und wurde in Couilly-Pont-aux-Dames, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guy II de Saint Pol è nato il nel 1196 nel Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Gaucher III de CHATILLON, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Elisabeth de SAINT-POL, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Agnès de Donzy II. Morì il 13 agosto 1226, nel Avignon, Vaucluse, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia, all'età di 30 anni, e fu sepolto a Couilly-Pont-aux-Dames, Seine-et-Marne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Guy II de Saint Polが1196年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスサイント-プオル-スル-テルノイゼで生まれた時，父Gaucher III de CHATILLONは30歳，母Elisabeth de SAINT-POLは16歳であった。彼はAgnès de Donzy IIとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1226年8月13日にフランスプローベンセ-アルペス-クoト ド'アズルヴァウクルゼアヴィグノンで30歳で死去し，フランスセアイネ-エト-マルンコウイリ-プオント-アウクス-ダムスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Guy II de Saint Pol(이)가 1196년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 세인트-포-수-터노이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gaucher III de CHATILLON(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elisabeth de SAINT-POL(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Agnès de Donzy II(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1226년 8월 13일 프랑스 플로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 바클루스 아비뇰에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 코우이리-폰트-오스-다메스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guy II de Saint Pol nasceu em 1196, em Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Gaucher III de CHATILLON, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth de SAINT-POL, tinha 16 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com Agnès de Donzy II. Ele faleceu em 13 de agosto de 1226, em Avignon, Vaucluse, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, França, com 30 anos, e foi sepultado em Couilly-Pont-aux-Dames, Seine-et-Marne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guy II de Saint Pol родился в 1196 г. в местности сеинт-пол-сур-тирноис, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Gaucher III de CHATILLON было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth de SAINT-POL, 16. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Agnès de Donzy II. Он умер 13 августа 1226 г. в местности авигнон, вауклус, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция в возрасте 30 лет и был похоронен в местности коуилли-понт-аукс-дэмесь, сине-ет-марн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guy II de Saint Pol nació en 1196, en Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Gaucher III de CHATILLON, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elisabeth de SAINT-POL, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con Agnès de Donzy II. Murió el 13 de agosto de 1226, en Avignon, Vaucluse, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia, a la edad de 30 años, y fue sepultado en Couilly-Pont-aux-Dames, Seine-et-Marne, Francia.
